search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng12 skin cancer pigmented lesion change size shape colour
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic melanoma diagnosis, biopsy, excision, or urgent referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The workflow does not contain all score inputs and no score was calculated.
All diagnosis and management decisions remain clinician-entered.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People reporting or observed to have a changing nevus or pigmented lesion; children, pregnancy, previous melanoma, familial syndromes, and immunosuppression need specialist context.
setting_applicability: UAE primary and specialist dermatology or skin-cancer assessment.
UAE_applicability: NICE recognition fields are transferable, but UAE cancer, dermoscopy, photography, pathology, consent, and referral pathways remain required.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG12 updated in 2026 and NICE NG14 updated in 2022 were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The reviewed NICE NG12 and NG14 recommendations are current.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact suspected-skin-cancer section supports documenting clinician-confirmed change in size, shape, colour, inflammation, oozing or sensation in a nevus without asserting melanoma or generating referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists suspicious pigmented-lesion features including size change, irregular shape or colour, diameter, inflammation, oozing and sensation change, plus dermoscopy and referral contexts for melanoma, squamous-cell carcinoma, and basal-cell carcinoma.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact specialist assessment section supports recording dermoscopy, baseline images and interval photographic comparison when actually performed; no diagnosis or excision decision is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The safety-net section supports documenting clinician-discussed warning changes and arranged re-evaluation without creating urgency or referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact lesion site, dimensions, morphology, dermoscopic findings, risk factors, skin type, sun exposure, prior images, diagnosis, biopsy or excision decision, pathology, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
